repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest repo entry is ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study, a static bounded-outcome wrapper

published_prior_art.cite: KalouptsidiKitamuraLimaSouzaRodrigues2020: dynamic counterfactual bounds by constrained optimization; theorem is mostly standard Markov-reward/support-function algebra

repo_prior_art.cite: closest banked entries pid_dynamic_iv_compliance, pid_long_term_surrogate, and pid_did_anticipation_bounded do not state a projective reversible-spell Cesaro/Hausdorff convergence theorem

published_prior_art.cite: Han2021 handles finite dynamic treatment-sequence identification; ChernozhukovFernandezValHahnNewey2013 and JunLeeShin2016 impose time-homogeneity/stationarity rather than proving finite-sharp-set Cesaro convergence

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no cited banked proposal has a face-cycling obstruction for long-run reversible-spell sharp intervals

published_prior_art.cite: HanYang2024, DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024, and KalouptsidiKitamuraLimaSouzaRodrigues2020 compute finite/discrete or structural bounds but are not shown to endorse finite-horizon-to-long-run extrapolation

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: HanYang2024 is listed with doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2023.105680, but the verified Journal of Econometrics article uses doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2024.105680.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The face-cycling obstruction names finite-bounds/computational papers but does not quote a specific published estimator or workflow that actually reports a long-run interval by extrapolating finite-horizon sharp endpoints.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption face stability says finite-horizon optimizers lie on the same exposed face as a stationary optimizer, but no projection/lifting map identifies faces of the finite compatibility polytopes with faces of the stationary reward correspondence.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define a precise face-comparison map, e.g. push finite latent laws to reward/occupation coordinates and state face stability as equality of exposed normal cones after that projection.

For Conjecture 2, target a named published extrapolation workflow and quote the exact step that treats finite dynamic bounds as a long-run estimand, or recast the result as a positive diagnostic rather than a refutation.
Add a small finite-state nondegenerate face-cycling template with varying observed outcomes in both sustained arms, so the obstruction is not perceived as a boundary artifact.

Add one mathematical prior-art paragraph on average-reward Markov decision/occupation-measure LP limits and parametric LP face stability, then explain why sharp causal compatibility constraints add something beyond that literature.

recommended_next_step: Revise the face-stability definition, fix the Han-Yang DOI, and retarget the face-cycling obstruction to a specific published extrapolation workflow before resubmitting.
